vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or dormant. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable plant, currently exhibiting severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil; no visible foliage.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains no identifiable mint foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf is present but shows progressive chlorosis and wilting.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, appears damp but lacks structural integrity; presence of white granular debris (likely eggshells) in P3.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_4: Pothos leaf remains stable; no change in soil.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Pothos leaf shows slight postural drooping.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_2: Pothos leaf exhibits further loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_T_minus_1: Significant lighting drop; plant appears to have collapsed or lost leaf surface visibility.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Total loss of visual detail due to extreme low light; plant is effectively invisible.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The Pothos (P3) is in a state of terminal decline, likely due to insufficient light and potential root-zone issues. The other pots are effectively empty or contain non-viable biological matter.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in P3; extreme lighting deficit in final frames preventing accurate assessment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The Pothos specimen, initially the only visible plant, has undergone a steady decline in turgor and vitality. The lack of light in the final two observations suggests a total breakdown of the monitoring environment, rendering the remaining specimens unobservable and likely non-viable.
